Name: Northeastern University Aerospace (AeroNU)
Relevance: Primary aerospace club managing competition rocket teams (NASA Student Launch, Spaceport America) and high-altitude balloon projects.

Name: Project Redshift
Relevance: Student-led team under AeroNU developing liquid-fueled sounding rockets for the Dollar-per-Foot (DPF) challenge.

Name: Northeastern University Rover Team (NU Rover)
Relevance: Develops Mars-style rovers to compete in the University Rover Challenge (URC) focusing on autonomous navigation and soil analysis.

Name: Northeastern Satellite Systems (NUSS)
Relevance: Formerly known as the CubeSat team; focuses on the design and integration of small satellites for NASA's CubeSat Launch Initiative.

Name: AstroNU
Relevance: Northeastern's undergraduate astronomy club focused on observation, telescope operation, and astrophysical workshops.

Name: SEDS Northeastern
Relevance: Northeastern chapter of Students for the Exploration and Development of Space focusing on space exploration advocacy and technology.

Name: Robotics and Intelligent Vehicles Research (RIVeR) Lab
Relevance: Conducts research on humanoid robotics (e.g., NASA's Valkyrie) for extreme environments including space exploration tasks.

Name: Wireless Networks and Embedded Systems (WIneS) Lab
Relevance: Researches terahertz communications and Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) including satellite and high-altitude platform connectivity.

Name: Aware Laboratory
Relevance: Investigates cognitive radio and wireless sensing systems frequently applied in aerial and orbital remote sensing platforms.

Name: Kostas Research Institute for Homeland Security (KRI)
Relevance: A major R&D hub for defense and aerospace technology, including uncrewed aerial systems (UAS) and resilient infrastructure.

Name: Institute for the Wireless Internet of Things (WIoT)
Relevance: Manages the Colosseum wireless testbed used for large-scale modeling of UAV networks and satellite constellation communications.

Name: Northeastern University Astrophysics Research Group
Relevance: A core research group in the Physics Department focusing on cosmology, dark matter surveys, and Large Scale Structure.

Name: High-Altitude Balloon Team (NU-HAB)
Relevance: Sub-group of AeroNU that designs high-altitude balloon payloads for atmospheric science and technology testing.

Name: Plasma Science and Engineering Research Group
Relevance: Researches plasma-material interactions and plasma physics relevant to spacecraft propulsion and thermal protection systems.

Name: Center for High-rate Nanomanufacturing (CHN)
Relevance: Develops advanced nanomanufacturing processes for high-performance materials used in aerospace and space-based electronics.

Name: Laboratory for Advanced Materials and Processes (LAMP)
Relevance: Researches additive manufacturing and materials for high-temperature applications like rocket engine components and turbine blades.

Name: NU-UAV
Relevance: Design team focused on the development of uncrewed aerial vehicles, autonomous flight controls, and aerial sensing.

Name: Northeastern University Experimental Rocketry
Relevance: A historical student group that merged into the current AeroNU rocket competition teams.

Name: Global Resilience Institute (GRI)
Relevance: Utilizes satellite data and remote sensing to assess global risks, infrastructure resilience, and climate impact.

Name: AIAA Northeastern Student Chapter
Relevance: The former name of the primary aerospace organization before restructuring into Northeastern Aerospace (AeroNU).
